{"step_by_step_thinking": "Cross-sectional studies are designed to capture data from a population at a single point in time. This means that the studies involve different individuals who are part of the same age group or age range, but not necessarily the same individuals at different points in time. The provided documents consistently describe cross-sectional studies as observing a snapshot of characteristics in a population at a single point in time, which aligns with option B.", "answer_choice": "B"}
